articles.title: Glucocorticoids for Patients With Acute Decompensated Heart Failure: A Protocol for a Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis and Trial Sequential Analysis
articles.summary: This protocol outlines a systematic review with meta-analysis and trial sequential analysis to evaluate the efficacy and safety of glucocorticoids in treating patients with acute decompensated heart failure. The study aims to synthesize evidence from randomized controlled trials to determine whether glucocorticoid therapy provides clinical benefits in this patient population. Trial sequential analysis will be employed to assess the robustness of findings and determine whether sufficient evidence exists to draw definitive conclusions.

articles.title: Highly sensitive m6A electrochemical biosensor based on laser-induced graphene electrodes functionalized with DNA tetrahedron/gold-antibody nanoparticle
articles.summary: Researchers developed a highly sensitive electrochemical biosensor for detecting m6A (N6-methyladenosine) using laser-induced graphene electrodes functionalized with DNA tetrahedron structures and gold-antibody nanoparticles. This biosensor platform combines electrochemical detection with nanomaterial-based signal amplification to achieve enhanced sensitivity for m6A biomarker analysis. The approach represents an advanced analytical tool for epigenetic modification detection with potential applications in biomarker-driven diagnostics.

articles.title: Specialist Inpatient Detoxification from Gabapentinoids: Clinical and Demographic Characteristics and Predictors of Outcome

The findings may inform clinical practice and patient selection for inpatient gabapentinoid withdrawal management programs.

articles.title: Diagnostic accuracy of synovial fluid progranulin vs. C-reactive protein in septic arthritis: a comparative biomarker study
articles.summary: This comparative biomarker study evaluated the diagnostic accuracy of synovial fluid progranulin versus C-reactive protein for identifying septic arthritis. The research directly compared these two biomarkers' performance as diagnostic tools in synovial fluid analysis. This work provides evidence on the relative utility of progranulin as an alternative or complementary biomarker to C-reactive protein in septic arthritis diagnosis.

articles.title: Patient-Specific Determinants of Response to BCMA- and GPRC5D-Targeted CAR T-Cell Therapy in Multiple Myeloma: A QSP Analysis of Clinical Trial and Real-World Data

The study applied computational modeling to characterize individual patient determinants of efficacy outcomes. Findings from this analysis may inform patient selection and treatment optimization strategies for CAR T-cell therapies in multiple myeloma.

articles.summary: This review examines the application of artificial intelligence in drug discovery and development, with a focus on oncology and hematology. The article discusses how AI technologies are being integrated into translational research and cancer therapeutics to advance the drug development pipeline. The work represents current perspectives on leveraging computational approaches to improve efficiency and outcomes in pharmaceutical research.

articles.title: Duplex Ultrasound Surveillance After Endovascular Therapy for Peripheral Arterial Disease: A Retrospective Cohort Study

The research was conducted at Prince of Wales Hospital in Sydney and involved investigators from the University of New South Wales. The study examines the role of post-treatment imaging surveillance in detecting complications and outcomes following endovascular intervention.

articles.title: A Hypoxia-Activated Supramolecular Albumin Conjugate for Tumor-Selective Chemotherapy via Host-Guest Recognition and Reductive Drug Release
